weather_report:
Subiaco sits under a clean, moon-brushed sky, the small hours hushed and glass-clear. Stars feel close enough to pocket, and the air has that crisp, uncomplicated edge that comes with cloudless winter nights. UV is essentially asleep at this hour, and the night reads as kind and uncomplicated, a good time for a quiet drive home or a last cup of tea by the window while the streetlights hum.

Dawn should break into a bright, unfiltered morning with excellent clarity and a mostly sunny day to follow. It will start cool, so step out in layers you can loosen once the sun gathers itself; a light jacket now, sunglasses and a touch of sunscreen by late morning when the UV wakes up, even in winter. It looks like a pleasant, practical day: a short walk to fetch bread, a ride to work without worrying about showers, windows opened to air the rooms, laundry pegged out to catch the gentle sun, maybe a midday pause on a bench to feel the light on your sleeves.
